channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Lebanon closes all its land border crossings with Syria except one
item.description: Lebanon is closing all its land border crossings with Syria except for a main one that links Beirut with the Syrian capital Damascus, the General Security Directorate said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court will decide if Palestinian authorities can be sued in US over attacks in Middle East
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to settle a years-long legal dispute over whether Palestinian authorities can be sued in U.S. courts by Americans killed or wounded in terrorism attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to consider reviving terror victims’ suits against Palestinian leadership
item.description: The Supreme Court said Friday it will consider upholding a law that allows Americans injured by acts of terror to take Palestinian leadership groups to U.S. courts for damages.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Closely-watched international climate case in the Hague wraps up its first week of testimony
item.description: A closely-watched international climate case that could yield guidance for governments around the world wrapped its first week of arguments before the top court of the United Nations in The Hague Friday.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkey’s Erdogan encourages Islamist rebels to continue advances as Assad regime scrambles to survive
item.description: Amid a fast moving seizure of Syrian cities by a U.S.-classified Islamist terrorist movement, the power politics of the Mideast is rapidly changing quickly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico study's surprising finding: Killer heat hit harder for the young than the elderly
item.description: A surprising study of temperature-related deaths in Mexico upends conventional thinking about what age group is hit hardest by heat.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of people fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday, positioning themselves for an assault on a potentially major prize in their march against President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame's reopening will begin with an archbishop's knock on the doors. Here's what comes next
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening this weekend of Notre Dame is a succession of ceremonies to breathe life back into the iconic cathedral and celebrate the recovery from its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: Romania's top court annulled the first round of the country's presidential election after intelligence was declassified alleging Russia interfered to promote Calin Georgescu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-British soldier pleads not guilty to 'Bloody Sunday' murders after judge clears the way for trial
item.description: A former British soldier will be tried for the murder of two men who were killed during the 1972 “Bloody Sunday” disturbances in Londonderry, Northern Ireland’s second-biggest city, after a judge refused to dismiss the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin signs agreement offering Russia's security guarantees to ally Belarus
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin visited Belarus on Friday to sign a treaty offering security guarantees for Moscow’s closest ally, including the possible use of Russian nuclear weapons to help repel an aggression.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Berlin's new panda twins have been named. Meet Leni and Lotti, or Meng Hao and Meng Tian
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's new panda twins now have names — or rather, two names apiece. The two female cubs, born at the Berlin Zoo on Aug. 22, were introduced Friday as Meng Hao and Meng Tian ("beautiful dreams" and “sweet dreams”).
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's governing party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, making impeachment more likely
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s governing party chief expressed support Friday for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law this week, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon’s impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ailing kids wait months for Israeli permission to leave Gaza for treatment. Some die in the meantime
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The 12-year-old Palestinian boy was lying in a hospital bed in central Gaza, wracked with leukemia, malnourished and whimpering in pain despite the morphine doctors were giving him, when Rosalia Bollen, a UNICEF official, said she saw him in late October.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where Turkey stands as opposition insurgents advance in neighboring Syria
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — As forces opposed to Syrian President Bashar Assad make swift and significant advances in Syria, Turkey is watching closely for any spillover.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: A top Romanian court has annulled the first round of the country’s presidential election, days after declassified intelligence alleged Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outsider who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia's LGBTQ+ community is living in fear following new laws and court rulings, activists say
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Gela Gogishvili and Haoyang Xu led a happy life as a gay couple in Russia, even as President Vladimir Putin’s government took an increasingly anti-LGBTQ+ stance.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about martial law and the effort to impeach South Korea's president
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is facing possible impeachment for sending heavily armed soldiers into Seoul's streets with a baffling declaration of martial law that reminded many of the country’s past military-backed dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French government collapses after no-confidence vote passes against Prime Minister Michel Barnier
item.description: Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to resign after the National Assembly passed a vote of no-confidence on Wednesday, effectively collapsing the current government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK killer nurse Letby questioned over further baby deaths
item.description: Lucy Letby, a former British nurse who is serving a life sentence for the murders of babies in her care, has reportedly been questioned about more infant deaths.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appoints former Middle East negotiator as hostage affairs envoy
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his selection of Adam Boehler to serve as special presidential envoy for hostage affairs in his second term. While the position holds the rank of an ambassador, it does not require Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Lawmakers see 'bad guys on both sides' in Syria offensive
item.description: Senators on both sides of the aisle are holding back from cheering rebel gains against Syrian President Bashar Assad, following a lightning offensive over the weekend led by Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), a U.S.-designated terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump's FBI pick Kash Patel targeted in Iranian cyberattack
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was among those targeted by an Iranian cyber attack. It’s unclear whether any information was gained through the attack, but it makes Patel the latest figure in the Trump campaign to be pursued by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Iranian activist, Nobel prize winner Mohammadi released from jail for medical treatment
item.description: Human rights activist Narges Mohammadi, who is imprisoned in Iran on charges including spreading propaganda, had her jail term suspended to undergo medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson rejects Biden request for $24 billion on new Ukraine aid
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson on Wednesday rejected a White House request to pass $24 billion in additional aid for Ukraine by the end of the year, saying any further assistance for Kyiv would be decided by President-elect Trump when he assumes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hispanic senators press Biden to protect immigrants before leaving White House
item.description: The Senate’s three current Hispanic Democrats are calling on President Biden to extend and strengthen humanitarian protections for certain groups of immigrants whose legal status could be stripped by the incoming Trump administration. In a letter on Wednesday, Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto (Nev.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Volkswagen workers hold strikes to push back against proposed pay cuts
item.description: Volkswagen workers launched rolling strikes at nine plants across Germany to underscore their resistance to pay cuts
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation
